pluginDescription: Controls Hamlib-compatible azimuth antenna rotators from the RadioControl toolbar.
setupHintDescription: Configure the rotator inside the RadioControl toolbar popup. The plugin uses the host hamlib dependency at runtime and stores rotator profiles in plugin global storage.
advancedConfigHint: One key=value per line, applied with setConf() before open().
presetsHint: Presets appear on the control tab for quick moves to common azimuths.
noElevation: Azimuth-only v1: elevation is never commanded and is sent as 0 when required by Hamlib.
firstMoveConfirm: The antenna is about to move. Confirm that the rotation area is clear of people, obstacles, and cable strain.
largeStepConfirm: Large movement detected. Confirm before sending this command.
parkConfirm: Park may move the antenna. Continue?
resetConfirm: Reset is a controller-level command. Continue only if you are sure.
staleConfirm: Hardware feedback is stale or unknown. Continue using the target angle anyway?
outOfLimit: Target is outside configured soft limits.
notConnected: Connect a rotator before sending movement commands.
hostHamlibMissing: Host hamlib dependency is not available. Make sure TX-5DR server includes hamlib@0.7.1.
hardware_position_outside_soft_limits: Hardware position is outside soft limits
networkBackend: Network mode always uses Hamlib / NET rotctl (#2) to connect to a remote rotctld endpoint, so no hardware model is selected here. Configure the hardware model on the rotctld side.
